challenge.title: Korak 42
challenge.description:
Koristeći istu logiku, kreiraj deklaraciju if koja se izvršava kada lista series nije prazna. Unutar nje, konkateniši niz === TV SERIES === (koji se završava znakom novog reda) sa trenutnom vrednošću result.

Zatim, kreiraj petlju for koja koristi enumeraciju za dodavanje stavki iz liste series promenljivoj result. Kao što si ranije uradio/uradila, počni indeksiranje vrednosti od 1.

Time je radionica medijskog kataloga završena.

challengeFiles.name: main

tests.text: You should have a second if statement in your __str__ method.
You should have result += '=== TV SERIES ===\n' inside your second if statement.
You should have a for loop in your second if statement.

When self.items contains Movie('Dances with Wolves', 1990, 'Kevin Costner', 224) and Movie('Annie Hall', 1977, 'Woody Allen', 93), your __str__ method should return Media Catalogue (2 items): === MOVIES === 1. Dances with Wolves (1990) - 224 min, Kevin Costner 2. Annie Hall (1977) - 93 min, Woody Allen .
tests.testString:
({
  test: () => runPython(`
    import io
    import sys
        
    captured_output = io.StringIO()
    sys.stdout = captured_output

    _catalogue = MediaCatalogue()
    _catalogue.add(Movie('Dances with Wolves', 1990, 'Kevin Costner', 224))
    _catalogue.add(Movie('Annie Hall', 1977, 'Woody Allen', 93))
    print(_catalogue)
        
    sys.stdout = sys.__stdout__
    output = captured_output.getvalue()

    _expected = """Media Catalogue (2 items):

    === MOVIES ===
    1. Dances with Wolves (1990) - 224 min, Kevin Costner
    2. Annie Hall (1977) - 93 min, Woody Allen
    """
    assert _expected in output
  `)
})

tests.text: When self.items contains TVSeries("Game of Thrones", 2011, "David Benioff & D.B. Weiss", 57, 8, 73) and TVSeries("Stranger Things", 2016, "The Duffer Brothers", 50, 4, 34), your __str__ method should return Media Catalogue (2 items): === TV SERIES === 1. Game of Thrones (2011) - 8 seasons, 73 episodes, 57 min avg, David Benioff & D.B. Weiss 2. Stranger Things (2016) - 4 seasons, 34 episodes, 50 min avg, The Duffer Brothers .
tests.testString:
({
  test: () => runPython(`
    import io
    import sys
        
    captured_output = io.StringIO()
    sys.stdout = captured_output

    _catalogue = MediaCatalogue()
    _catalogue.add(TVSeries("Game of Thrones", 2011, "David Benioff & D.B. Weiss", 57, 8, 73))
    _catalogue.add(TVSeries("Stranger Things", 2016, "The Duffer Brothers", 50, 4, 34))
    print(_catalogue)
        
    sys.stdout = sys.__stdout__
    output = captured_output.getvalue()

    _expected = """Media Catalogue (2 items):

    === TV SERIES ===
    1. Game of Thrones (2011) - 8 seasons, 73 episodes, 57 min avg, David Benioff & D.B. Weiss
    2. Stranger Things (2016) - 4 seasons, 34 episodes, 50 min avg, The Duffer Brothers
    """
    assert _expected in output
  `)
})

tests.text: When self.items contains Movie('Barry Lyndon', 1975, 'Stanley Kubrick', 184), Movie('Fahrenheit 451', 1966, 'Francois Truffaut', 112), TVSeries("Game of Thrones", 2011, "David Benioff & D.B. Weiss", 57, 8, 73) and TVSeries("Stranger Things", 2016, "The Duffer Brothers", 50, 4, 34) your __str__ method should return Media Catalogue (4 items): === MOVIES === 1. Barry Lyndon (1975) - 184 min, Stanley Kubrick 2. Fahrenheit 451 (1966) - 112 min, Francois Truffaut === TV SERIES === 1. Game of Thrones (2011) - 8 seasons, 73 episodes, 57 min avg, David Benioff & D.B. Weiss 2. Stranger Things (2016) - 4 seasons, 34 episodes, 50 min avg, The Duffer Brothers .
tests.testString:
({
  test: () => runPython(`
    import io
    import sys
        
    captured_output = io.StringIO()
    sys.stdout = captured_output

    _catalogue = MediaCatalogue()
    _catalogue.add(Movie('Barry Lyndon', 1975, 'Stanley Kubrick', 184))
    _catalogue.add(Movie('Fahrenheit 451', 1966, 'Francois Truffaut', 112))
    _catalogue.add(TVSeries("Game of Thrones", 2011, "David Benioff & D.B. Weiss", 57, 8, 73))
    _catalogue.add(TVSeries("Stranger Things", 2016, "The Duffer Brothers", 50, 4, 34))
    print(_catalogue)
        
    sys.stdout = sys.__stdout__
    output = captured_output.getvalue()

    _expected = """Media Catalogue (4 items):

    === MOVIES ===
    1. Barry Lyndon (1975) - 184 min, Stanley Kubrick
    2. Fahrenheit 451 (1966) - 112 min, Francois Truffaut
    === TV SERIES ===
    1. Game of Thrones (2011) - 8 seasons, 73 episodes, 57 min avg, David Benioff & D.B. Weiss
    2. Stranger Things (2016) - 4 seasons, 34 episodes, 50 min avg, The Duffer Brothers
    """
    assert _expected in output
  `)
})

tests.text: Your __str__ method should return Media Catalogue (empty) when self.items is empty.
tests.testString:
({
  test: () => runPython(`
    import io
    import sys
        
    captured_output = io.StringIO()
    sys.stdout = captured_output
        
    print(MediaCatalogue())
        
    sys.stdout = sys.__stdout__
    output = captured_output.getvalue()
        
    assert "Media Catalogue (empty)" in output
  `)
})
